unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns the words for family members (mum, dad, sister, brother, grandma, grandpa), some breakfast and fruit words, and how to count from 1 to 10. They talk about who is in their family and how families help and have fun together. The gentle idea is that families love and help each other. The goal is warm, confident talking in English — not perfect spelling.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Name the people in a family (mother, father, sister, brother, grandma, grandpa, baby).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Name some breakfast foods and fruits (pancakes, cereal, milk, mango, banana, grapes).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Count from 1 to 10, and count backwards from 10 to 1.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Ask and answer simple questions: “Do you like bananas?” → “Yes, I do.” / “No, I don't.”
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say what their family does together (help, work, play, eat, talk, laugh).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Hear the short ‘a’ sound in words like cat, hat and map.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

readings.title: Breakfast at Grandma's House
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
It was Saturday morning. Amal woke up early. “We are going to Grandma's house!” she said.

Selma has a big family. She has a mother and a father. She has a little brother named Ishmael. Ishmael is a baby. He is very small.

“Come, Amal,” said her mum. “Hold my hand.” They walked to Grandma's house together.

Grandma opened the door. “Hello, my dear ones!” she said. She gave everyone a big hug.

“I love you, Grandma,” said Amal.

Grandpa was in the kitchen. “Good morning!” he said. “Who is hungry? It is time to eat!”

“Me! Me!” said Amal. She was very hungry.

On the table there was lots of fruit. There was one mango. There was one yellow banana. There were ten green grapes. There were four red strawberries.

“Let us count the grapes,” said Grandpa. Amal counted: “one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten!” Ten grapes!

“Well done, Amal!” said Grandpa.

“Can I help?” asked Amal. “Yes, please,” said Grandma. “You can lay the table.” Amal put out the little bowls. One, two, three, four, five bowls.

Everyone sat down. They ate the sweet mango. They ate the soft banana. Baby Ishmael ate his cereal with milk. Some of it went on his nose! Everyone began to laugh.

“Ishmael is a funny baby,” said Amal, and she gave him a kiss.

After breakfast, the family sat together. Mum and Dad talked with Grandma and Grandpa. Amal helped to tidy the bowls. Then she played a counting game with Ishmael.

“I love Saturday,” said Amal. “I love my family best of all.”

Grandma smiled. “And your family loves you, little one,” she said.

readings.title: Talk about Family Time
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. This is my ___. He is my ___. She is my ___. Do you like ___? There are ___ ___. I can help by ___. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: Family Time rhyme
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
The “Families” poem — say it together
Some families are big,  /  Some families are small.  /  I love my family best of all.
After you say it, ask your child: “Is our family big or small? Who is in our family?” Say the poem three times — a little louder each time!
The “Ten in the Bed” counting song
There are 10 in the bed  /  And the little one says, ‘Roll over. Roll over.’  /  So they all roll over and 1 falls out…  (count down to 1)  /  There is 1 in the bed  /  And the little one says, ‘Good night!’
Hold up ten fingers and put one down each time. This is a fun way to practise counting backwards.

grammar.title: Language pattern 1
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: This is my ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: This is my ___.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: This is my ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern This is my ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: He is my ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern He is my ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern She is my ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Do you like ___? Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern There are ___ ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw one idea about family, food, counting and helping. Tell an adult what it shows.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace two useful words, saying each sound or word as you trace.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy
writing.promptAndInstructions: Match three words to pictures, then copy one word.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your drawing or picture.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose a picture and complete one short sentence using: I can help by ___.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

assignments.title: My Family Time show-and-tell page
assignments.instructions: Submit one drawing or made object, two labels or traced words, one recorded spoken pattern and an adult-supported reflection.
assignments.submissionType: Drawing or object + labels + short audio/video + reflection

liveSessions.title: Meet the words
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring crayons and one real object connected to the Unit.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from Family Time to an adult.

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name the people in a family (mother, father, sister, brother, grandma, grandpa, baby).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can name some breakfast foods and fruits (pancakes, cereal, milk, mango, banana, grapes).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can count from 1 to 10, and count backwards from 10 to 1.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can ask and answer simple questions: “Do you like bananas?” → “Yes, I do.” / “No, I don't.”.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say what their family does together (help, work, play, eat, talk, laugh).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can hear the short ‘a’ sound in words like cat, hat and map.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself
